How many medals did Team GB win at the Paris Olympics?

Removed from bookmarks

I would like to be emailed about offers, events and updates from The Independent. Read ourPrivacy notice

Team GBhave concluded a strongParis 2024with 65 medals, one more thanGreat Britainachieved at the TokyoOlympics, to finish sixth in the final medal table.

Officials had set a target of between 50 and 70 medals which was safely achieved as a number of big stars secured Olympic triumph, includingKeely Hodgkinson on the Stade de France track, Alex Yeein a dramatic men’s triathlonand three crews on the water at Vaires-sur-Marne as therowers rebounded from a difficult time in Tokyo.

Great Britain did fall short of their tally of 22 golds from the last Summer Games, winning 14, with a series ofnear misses in the pooland in the athletics programme capping their top of the podium potential and ultimately meaning a drop of two places in the overall table.

Yet history was made as first medals werewon in climbing and artistic swimming, showcasing the pan-sport competitiveness of the British squad, while Emma Finucane was an individual standout,winning three medals at the velodrome.

Here is a day-by-day recap of all of Team GB’s medals in Paris.

Anna Henderson(cycling, women’s road time trial) 🥈

Adam Peaty(swimming, men’s 100 metre breaststroke) 🥈
